name: Laguna de El Oso Loop
name_original: Lagunas de La Moraña — circular desde El Oso
description: Hike the 5.6-mile Lagunas de La Moraña circular trail from El Oso, a moderate route through wetlands perfect for birdwatching.
region_intro: Hiking around El Oso offers routes through the flat plains and gentle hills of the Castilian plateau in Spain. The landscape is characterized by agricultural fields, open countryside, and the presence of lagoons. Trails often follow rural paths, providing views of the expansive Spanish countryside. Best hiking trails around El Oso The most popular hiking route is Castro de las Cogotas Circular Trail, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 15 minutes to complete. This moderate route features gentle elevation changes across open terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Yacimiento de las Cogotas — circular desde Cardeñosa, a moderate 10.1 miles (16.2 km) path. This trail explores the historical site of Cogotas, offering views of the surrounding plains. Local hikers also love the Laguna de El Oso Loop, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail leading through open countryside and past the Laguna de El Oso, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around El Oso is defined by open plains, agricultural landscapes, and the occasional lagoon, with routes offering options for various ability levels from easy to difficult. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 4 hikers have used komoot to explore El Oso's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscape can I expect when hiking around El Oso?
A: Hiking around El Oso offers routes through the flat plains and gentle hills of the Castilian plateau in Spain. The landscape is characterized by agricultural fields, open countryside, and the presence of lagoons, providing expansive views of the Spanish countryside.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in El Oso?
A: There are over 10 hiking routes available around El Oso, catering to various skill levels from easy to difficult.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for beginners in El Oso?
A: Yes, El Oso offers at least one easy hiking route. Many of the trails feature gentle elevation changes across open terrain, making them accessible for those new to hiking or looking for a relaxed walk.

Q: Are there any circular hikes around El Oso?
A: Yes, several popular routes are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1325244174/castro-de-las-cogotas-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de las Cogotas Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.4-mile (11.9 km) option, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249108582/laguna-de-el-oso-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de El Oso Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 5.6-mile (9.0 km) trail leading through open countryside.

Q: What are some longer or more challenging hikes in the El Oso area?
A: For a more challenging experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1250106622/gotarrendura-and-el-oso-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gotarrendura and El Oso Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 14.5-mile (23.4 km) route. Another moderate but longer option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1137612105/yacimiento-de-las-cogotas-circular-desde-cardenosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yacimiento de las Cogotas — circular desde Cardeñosa&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 10.1 miles (16.2 km).

Q: What can I see along the hiking trails around El Oso?
A: The trails often explore historical sites like Cogotas and pass by natural features such as the Laguna de El Oso. You can also find interesting landmarks nearby, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3704019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Narros de Saldueña&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5987624" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Pedro Apóstol&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is El Oso suitable for family-friendly hikes?
A: Given the prevalence of flat plains and gentle hills, many of the easier and moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249108582/laguna-de-el-oso-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de El Oso Loop&lt;/a&gt;, are suitable for families looking for a pleasant walk in the countryside.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in El Oso?
A: The Castilian plateau generally experiences hot summers and cold winters. Spring and autumn are typically the most pleasant seasons for hiking, offering milder temperatures and comfortable conditions for exploring the open countryside.

Q: Are there any lagoons or water features to explore while hiking?
A: Yes, the region is known for the presence of lagoons. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249108582/laguna-de-el-oso-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de El Oso Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically leads past the Laguna de El Oso, offering views of this natural water feature.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in El Oso?
A: Hikers often appreciate the open plains, agricultural landscapes, and the quiet, expansive Spanish countryside. The routes provide a good mix of historical interest and natural beauty, with options for various ability levels.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in El Oso?
A: Generally, rural trails in Spain, especially those through open countryside and agricultural areas, are dog-friendly. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs under control, especially near livestock, and to check for any specific local regulations or signage on individual trails.
tips: The museum allows visitors to explore the cultural and natural heritage of El Oso while enjoying a walk in the open air. It is a great way to learn more about the history and traditions of this small municipality in Ávila. 🌄😎🚶‍♂️

Opened in 2017, the center offers a variety of educational and recreational activities, including guided tours, real-time bird watching via video cameras, and exhibits on local fauna and flora.
The center also features dioramas, a screening room, and an observation deck, providing an immersive experience for visitors.

This is the main lagoon in this area, of the steppe type. It is not very deep, since in none of its points does it exceed 70 centimeters.
It is of great interest to ornithology lovers, as it has great faunal value, hosting a good number of wintering waterfowl and being a stopover for migratory birds such as waders or spoonbills.

The Church of San Pedro Apóstol in El Oso, Ávila, is a parish church dating back to the 16th century. It stands out for its stone architecture, with three naves separated by large semicircular arches and a 17th-century Baroque main altarpiece. ⛪😎
In front of the church, there is the Stone Bear, also known as Verraco Vettón. This stone sculpture is part of the town's Open Air Museum and has great historical and cultural importance.🐻

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Lagunas de La Moraña — circular desde El Oso trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as moderate. While it doesn't feature steep climbs, it requires a reasonable level of physical fitness to complete the nearly 9-kilometer distance.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Lagunas de La Moraña trail?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily consists of easy paths, characteristic of the plains and wetlands of La Moraña. You can expect mostly flat ground with minimal elevation changes as the route winds around the lagoons and through cereal fields.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The area is a crucial wetland with exceptional ornithological value. You can expect to see large concentrations of overwintering aquatic birds, including common cranes, and various steppe birds like the Great Bustard. It's also a good spot for raptors, mammals, amphibians, reptiles, and insects.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Lagunas de La Moraña trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is ideal for birdwatching throughout the year, with peak times during winter and the spring and autumn migrations. These seasons offer the best opportunities to observe the diverse birdlife.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Lagunas de La Moraña — circular desde El Oso trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in El Oso. Parking is generally available within the village, which serves as the main access point for the route.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Lagunas de La Moraña?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Reserva Ornitológica de Las Lagunas de El Oso. While there are no general entrance fees for hiking the trail itself, it's always advisable to check for any specific regulations or guided visit requirements, especially if you plan to access dedicated observation points or participate in environmental education programs.

faqs.question: Is the Lagunas de La Moraña trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Given the area's importance as a bird sanctuary, dogs are generally allowed but must be kept on a leash at all times to protect the local wildlife and ensure a peaceful experience for all visitors. Please clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or features will I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll pass by the Tree of Life of El Oso, the Interpretation Center of the Lagunas de la Moraña, and the Laguna de El Oso itself. The village also features the Church of San Pedro Apóstol and the Verraco Vettón (Stone Bear).

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with any other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this circular route overlap with other official trails. You will find parts of the Ruta Teresiana, Ruta del Sureste, Carretera de Sanchidrian, and Camino a Gotarrendura along the way.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like restrooms or an interpretation center along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Interpretation Center of the Lagunas de la Moraña is located along the route, offering insights into the ecosystem and potentially providing facilities. It's a good place to learn more about the wetlands.

faqs.question: Are there options for food or accommodation near El Oso?
faqs.answer: The village of El Oso, where the trail starts, offers local amenities. While specific establishments aren't detailed, it's a small community, so checking local listings for cafes, pubs, or accommodation in advance is recommended.
